bead.title: agent: wrap artifact bodies with untrusted-data delimiters
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.
- Do not rewrite unrelated server text egress paths.
bead.acceptance:
1. A reusable delimiter helper exists for untrusted artifact bodies.
2. Prompt assembly uses the helper at every artifact-body interpolation site in scope.
3. `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` proves the delimiter contract and payload preservation.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

The current tree already contains the reusable `evidence.DelimitUntrustedData` helper and the review prompt uses it for governing-doc and diff artifact bodies, so there is no scoped code change to make for this bead. The required verification gate `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...` is failing in pre-existing `internal/server` and `internal/server/graphql` retry-threshold tests (`TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigServer`, `TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigGraphQL`), which blocks proving acceptance item 4 without unrelated fixes.

The requested helper and regression test are already present in the current tree (`cli/internal/evidence/untrusted.go` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_evidence_test.go`), so no code change was needed for the delimiter contract itself. However, the required verification command `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...` does not pass in this baseline: `internal/agent` fails in pre-existing cancellation/probe tests (`TestWorker_HonorsCancelMidAttempt`, `TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA`) and `internal/agent/workerprobe` fails on VCS stamping (`go build ddx: error obtaining VCS status`). Those failures block a clean completion signal for the bead without stepping outside the bead scope.

rationale: All acceptance criteria for ddx-d6730314 are already satisfied by prior commit
889fe4552 ("fix: delimit untrusted artifact bodies [ddx-0e5c3005]").

AC1: cli/internal/evidence/untrusted.go — DelimitUntrustedData helper with UntrustedDataOpen/Close constants.
AC2: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:375 and :397 — both artifact-body interpolation sites (governing docs and diff) use evidence.DelimitUntrustedData.
AC3: TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_evidence_test.go — passes, asserts delimiter wrapping and payload preservation.
AC4: cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/... — all pass.
AC5: lefthook run pre-commit — passes.

No code changes are needed; bead is complete.
